candidates.name: Brian Schwalb
candidates.notes:
Took office Jan 2023. Running for re-election 2026.

[2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (oag.dc.gov). Classic blue-state-AG profile — uniformly progressive on substance, with the additional wrinkle that as DC AG every 'federal overreach' lawsuit (sanctuary, MPD takeover, National Guard removal, tax-cut override) is procedurally TRUE-direction on a Home-Rule axis but SUBSTANTIVELY FALSE on the rubric (because what he's defending is sanctuary, cashless bail, noncitizen voting, gun control). Score against substance per rubric directions. HARD-FALSE: (life q1) joined 23-AG mifepristone amicus in *Alliance for Hippocratic Medicine v. FDA* + co-led 18-AG *Bryant v. Stein* mifepristone coalition + sued Easy Healthcare to protect reproductive data; (family q1) joined CA-led 16-AG amicus defending Chico Unified school policy withholding gender-identity info from parents; (election q1) defended DC's noncitizen-voting Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 in litigation through DC Circuit (June 2025 reversal for standing); (public_justice q1) joint statement w/ Mayor Bowser & Chair Mendelson opposing Congressional bills to end DC cashless bail — 'cash bail creates a two-tiered justice system'; (public_justice q3) OAG routinely frames youth crime via 'trauma, behavioral health support, mentoring, family therapy' through ACE diversion program — explicit public-health rehabilitation paradigm; (border q1) rejected AG Bondi's order requiring DC police cooperation with ICE as 'unlawful'; (border q2) OAG operates 'Defending Our Immigrant Community' program + Jan 24 2025 guidance restricting ICE access to DC schools; (self_defense q0) led 18-AG coalition defending Maryland sensitive-places carry restrictions in *Kipke/Novotny v. Moore* + defends DC firearms-on-transit ban; (self_defense q1) defends DC large-capacity-magazine prohibition + co-led 24-AG VanDerStok ghost-gun amicus (7-2 upheld March 26 2025). NULL: life q0; marriage q0/q2/q4 (only indirect coalition signal, no DC-issued release on Skrmetti specifically); family q0/q2; christian_liberty all 4 (no record on 303 Creative/Masterpiece Cakeshop/FFRF — the 'Brian L. Schwalb' on those filings is unrelated counsel); economic_stewardship q2; election q0/q4; public_justice q0 (Home-Rule defense of MPD chief is SOFT, not HIGH-BAR pro-LE), q2, q4 (mixed sword/diversion); refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — his Home Rule litigation is anti-federal, not anti-state).

candidates.name: Anita Bonds
candidates.notes:
Not running for re-election 2026.

[2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (dccouncil.gov/council/anita-bonds). Mainstream institutional DC Democrat — uniformly progressive on abortion/LGBTQ/sanctuary/noncitizen-voting (HARD-FALSE Pillar A + immigration). TWO cross-pressure HARD-TRUEs worth noting: (1) **economic_stewardship q2 HARD-TRUE** — one of only 5 NO votes against the 2021 amendment to FY2022 Budget Support Act raising taxes on residents earning >$250K (8-5 vote July 20 2021), framing protection of middle-income property/bond-holders; (2) **public_justice q4 HARD-TRUE** — voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus 2024 (B25-0345) expanding police authority, drug-free zones, pretrial detention: 'This bill is monumental in its attempt to make us all feel safer.' Also (public_justice q2 SOFT-TRUE) — introduced (later withdrawn) Secure DC amendment expanding 'crime of violence' definition for senior victims. Pillar A: HARD-FALSE life q0/q1 — co-introduced D.C. Law 23-90 Reproductive Health Protections (2020) AND D.C. Law 24-254 Enhanced Reproductive Health Protections (2022) shielding self-managed abortion and providers. HARD-FALSE marriage q4 — cosponsored B25-0298 Black LGBTQ History Preservation Establishment Amendment Act mandating LGBTQIA+ Affairs research/report. Election integrity q1 FALSE (SOFT) — Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act 24-242 (noncitizen voting in DC local elections) passed Oct 2022 on near-unanimous Council votes; no record of Bonds dissenting and she chairs Council Operations w/ election focus. Public_justice q1 FALSE (SOFT) — backed RCCA veto override 12-1 (Jan 2023), called it 'very good bill.' Border q1/q2 FALSE (HARD) — voted YES Sanctuary Values Amendment Act of 2020 (D.C. Law 23-282, 13-0). NULL: christian_liberty all 4 (no public profession of Christ on record); marriage q0/q2 (only SOFT third-party characterizations, no own quote); family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (DC OSP is federally funded, no Bonds vote/statement located); election q0/q4; public_justice q0 (no clean 'back the blue' statement — HIGH BAR not met) and q3; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17); self_defense q0/q1 (no specific Bonds vote located on the 2018 ERPO/red-flag or AWB).

profile.background: DC Council At-Large since Dec 11 2012; former DC Democratic Party chair (2006-2018); long career in Marion Barry's orbit; chairs Housing committee. Elected Council Chair pro tempore Jan 20 2026 (succeeded Kenyan McDuffie). Announced Dec 7 2025 will NOT seek 2026 reelection — serves through current term (Jan 2027).

candidates.name: Brooke Pinto
candidates.notes:
Running for DC Delegate to Congress 2026. Raised $843K+.

[2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (brookepintodc.com). HEADLINE CROSS-PARTY CATCH: D Judiciary chair with a public-safety-pragmatist record that earns FOUR HARD-TRUE public_justice cells against a uniformly progressive social-issue voting record. HARD-TRUE Pillar B public-justice cluster: (q0) authored Secure DC Omnibus (drug-free zones, mask ban, neck restraints, high-speed chases) — affirmative pro-LE program; (q1) Secure DC creates rebuttable presumption in favor of pretrial detention for juveniles charged with violent crimes; (q2) FY26 Judiciary budget funded ALL of MPD's request + $2.5M training-academy capital + $2.4M Police & Fire Clinic + $2.3M 911/311 raises; Peace DC plan explicitly increases MPD/DOC/DYRS hiring; (q3) was 3-vote minority pushing HIGHER gun-penalty maxes during RCCA process: 'important that judges have more discretion for these penalties'; (q4) Secure DC makes permanent felony of endangerment-with-a-firearm, prohibits gun possession by stalking convictees, raises max gun-crime sentences 1→5 years. TRUE-SOFT econ q2: publicly opposed BOTH FY25 employer payroll-tax increase AND residential property-tax increase on homes >$2.5M ('neither of which Pinto supported'), though still voted final budget package. HARD-FALSE social cluster: (life q0) co-introduced 4 bills shielding/expanding abortion post-Dobbs incl. Enhancing Reproductive Health Protections + insurer cost-sharing mandate; (marriage q0) co-introduced William Dorsey Swann Street Designation Act 2023 + funded $1M LGBTQQIA+ Community Center + $479K LGBTQ senior housing vouchers; (marriage q2) explicit support for 'trans women of color' protections, Bella Evangelista/Tony Hunter Panic Defense Prohibition Act 2020; (election q1) CO-INTRODUCED Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 allowing noncitizens (incl. undocumented) to vote — 'true representation requires that all of our residents' voices are heard'; (self_defense q0) authored expansion of DC firearm offenses + increased penalties + new crime for firing gun in public; (self_defense q1) voted YES Nov 16 2021 DC ghost-gun definition expansion + publicly advocates 'tamping down ghost guns.' SOFT-FALSE: (border q1) defended DC sanctuary status vs House disapproval 'trample our right to Home Rule' but did NOT author original Sanctuary Values Act (predated her chairmanship); (christian_liberty q2) led 2021 'COVID Recovery and Vaccination Plan' briefing — supportive of mandates regime, no opposition recorded → left NULL in dossier per high-bar discipline. NULL: life q1; marriage q4; family_child_sovereignty all 3; christian_liberty all 4; election q0/q4; border q2; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17). Net profile: PUBLIC-SAFETY-PRAGMATIST DEMOCRAT with progressive social-issue priors and one HARD-TRUE fiscal cross-pressure — the cleanest cross-party catch in this batch.

profile.background: DC Council Ward 2 since June 27 2020. Chair, Committee on the Judiciary and Public Safety (since Dec 21 2022). Announced Oct 6 2025 as candidate for DC Delegate to Congress (2026).
